# Kool Codez

> Frontend engineering by Lucio D'Alessandro. Open source React libraries and TypeScript UI components focused on speed, polish, and long term maintainability.

Kool Codez is the personal engineering site of Lucio D'Alessandro, a senior frontend contractor with a decade of experience shipping React and TypeScript UIs in production, including at a global investment bank. The site publishes open source libraries and long form technical writing.

## Libraries

- [react-socket](https://koolcodez.com/projects/react-socket): Type safe React hooks for real time WebSocket communication. Automatic reconnection, optimistic updates, offline message queuing. Published on npm as `@luciodale/react-socket`. Source: https://github.com/luciodale/react-socket
- [react-searchable-dropdown](https://koolcodez.com/projects/react-searchable-dropdown): High performance virtualized React dropdown and combobox. Single and multi selection, async search, custom rendering, create new options, zero dependencies, accessible. Published on npm as `@luciodale/react-searchable-dropdown`. Source: https://github.com/luciodale/react-searchable-dropdown
- [swipe-bar](https://koolcodez.com/projects/swipe-bar): Gesture driven React sidebar with spring physics. Touch and mouse, native app feel for mobile first web interfaces, tiny footprint. Published on npm as `@luciodale/swipe-bar`. Source: https://github.com/luciodale/swipe-bar
- [fork](https://koolcodez.com/projects/fork): Headless form management for Re-frame and Reagent. Declarative validation, dirty tracking, submission handling for ClojureScript. Published on Clojars as `fork`. Source: https://github.com/luciodale/fork

## Articles

- [WebSockets Are Back. The Client Side needs more support.](https://koolcodez.com/blog/websockets-are-back): Why bidirectional streaming became essential again, and the motivation behind react-socket.
- [How Close Can a PWA Get to a Native App?](https://koolcodez.com/blog/how-close-can-a-pwa-get-to-a-native-app): A hands on demo exploring how far the web can go before a native rewrite becomes justified.
- [Inside a 16.67 Millisecond Frame](https://koolcodez.com/blog/inside-the-frame): Practical walkthrough of how the browser renders within a single frame budget.
- [From Spaghetti to Simplicity](https://koolcodez.com/blog/from-spaghetti-to-simplicity): Rebuilding a legacy trading UI at a global bank.
- [Trading Grids: A Technical Deep Dive](https://koolcodez.com/blog/trading-grid): Production ready financial data grids handling real time market data and complex interaction.
- [10 CSS Features I've Found Really Useful](https://koolcodez.com/blog/useful-css-features): Lesser known CSS properties that solve common UI problems cleanly.
- [We Badly Need Frameworks](https://koolcodez.com/blog/we-badly-need-frameworks): An honest look at the tradeoffs of modern frontend frameworks.
